hooks.command: FILEPATH="$TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH$TOOL_INPUT_file_path"; case "$FILEPATH" in *.env*|*credentials*|*secrets*|*.pem|*.key|*.p12) echo '{"decision":"block","reason":"Sensitive file — requires explicit user confirmation before writing"}' ;; *) echo '{"decision":"allow"}' ;; esac

FILEPATH="$TOOL_INPUT_FILE_PATH$TOOL_INPUT_file_path"; case "$FILEPATH" in *coding-agents-practice-guide*) echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"additionalContext":"CONTENT RULES: Every feature claim must be verifiable against official docs. Comparative tables must be fair to both agents. No stub sections. Include Mermaid diagrams where they clarify architecture or flow."}}' ;; *AGENTS.md|*MASTER_PLAN*) echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"additionalContext":"PROJECT DOCS: Ensure consistency with docs/status.md and docs/versions.md. Phase constraints must be respected."}}' ;; *) echo '{}' ;; esac

cd /Users/amrambouskila/IMPORTANT/Projects/coding-agents-tooling && CHANGES=$(git status --porcelain 2>/dev/null | head -20); if [ -n "$CHANGES" ]; then echo "{\"systemMessage\":\"UNCOMMITTED CHANGES in coding-agents-tooling:\n$(echo $CHANGES | head -10)\nRemember: you manage git yourself.\"}"; else echo '{}'; fi

echo '{"systemMessage":"SESSION END — MEMORY REVIEW: Before this session closes, review what was learned and suggest memories the user may want to save. Check ~/.codex/memories/-Users-amrambouskila-IMPORTANT-Projects-coding-agents-tooling/memory/MEMORY.md first to avoid duplicates — update existing memories rather than creating new ones when the topic already exists. Memory types: user (role, preferences, knowledge), feedback (corrections given, confirmed approaches — include WHY), project (decisions with external motivation, deadlines, stakeholder context), reference (pointers to external systems). Worth saving: corrections the user gave you, non-obvious approaches they confirmed, project decisions driven by external factors, pointers to external tools/systems, user preferences learned this session. NOT worth saving: anything derivable from code or git history, debugging solutions, ephemeral task details, things already in AGENTS.md. Present a short bulleted list of suggested memories at the end of your response and let the user decide which to save. Memory dir: ~/.codex/memories/-Users-amrambouskila-IMPORTANT-Projects-coding-agents-tooling/memory/"}'
